basics.name: Anthony Nduka
basics.label: Catholic Priest | Data Scientist | Human-Centered AI Researcher
basics.summary: Catholic priest and data scientist with a strong background in AI, healthcare analytics, and ethical computing. Proven experience in interdisciplinary research, academic teaching, and technology solutions for underserved populations. Committed to advancing human-centered AI and socially impactful technologies.

work.name: Spiritan University, Nneochi
work.summary: Support teaching, research, and mentoring in data science and human-centered computing, with focus on ethical and accessible technologies.
highlights: Mentored undergraduate students on data projects and coursework.
Supported instructional delivery for Python and data science classes.
Provided grading and academic feedback to students.

work.name: Spiritan Healthcare Apostolate
work.summary: Lead data-driven healthcare initiatives for elderly and vulnerable populations, integrating ethics, technology, and pastoral care in a real-world mission setting.
highlights: Developed predictive healthcare models for proactive elder care.
Digitized medical records to improve service coordination.
Over 155 elderly served across four Nigerian dioceses.

work.name: Spiritan Provincialate, Onitsha
work.summary: Manage the province’s administrative data systems, support strategic planning, and coordinate digital record-keeping with a focus on efficiency, privacy, and ethical data practices.
highlights: Built and maintained a centralized database for personnel and provincial operations.
Recognized by the Spiritan Generalate, Rome, as Best Data Management Secretary in 2022.
Supported digitization of archival and governance documents across the province.
Collaborated with technical teams to ensure secure data handling and storage.

work.summary: Lead organizer for the New York City branch of the People's Climate March, the largest climate march in history.
highlights: Awarded 'Climate Hero' award by Greenpeace for my efforts organizing the march.
Men of the year 2014 by Time magazine

awards.title: Most Efficient Data Management Secretary
awards.summary: Awarded for excellence in provincial database administration.

awards.title: Best Paper Presenter
awards.awarder: National Association of Catholic Theology Students (NACATHS)
awards.summary: Awarded for outstanding paper presentation on Cyber Evangelization.

publications.name: Zur Elektrody/namik bewegter Körper
publications.summary: It concerned an interpretation of the Michelson–Morley experiment and the properties of light and time. Special relativity incorporates the principle that the speed of light is the same for all inertial observers regardless of the state of motion of the source.

publications.name: Leveraging Big Data and IoT in Media Industry
publications.publisher: Under Review at Science and Technology Publishing
publications.summary: Explores the use of data and IoT to transform content creation, distribution, and audience engagement.

publications.name: Quantum Computing in Healthcare
publications.publisher: Under Review at International Journal of Innovative Science and Research Technology
publications.summary: Explores how quantum computing supports diagnostics, drug discovery, and personalized medicine.

projects.name: Quantum Computing
projects.summary: Quantum computing is the use of quantum-mechanical phenomena such as superposition and entanglement to perform computation. Computers that perform quantum computations are known as quantum computers.
